content.full_text:
Re:
Fnrn PoLrrrcAL PRACTTcES Corr,rrrars sroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
December 30,2002
Andrea M. Chelemengos, City Clerk
City of Monte Sereno
18041 Saratoga-Los Gatos Road
Monte Sereno, CA 95030
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-02-332
Dear Ms. Chelemengos:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the City of Monte
Sereno Site and Architecture Commission regarding the statement of economic interests
filing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the.Act,).r
QUESTTON
1. Are members of Monte sereno's newly created Site and Architectural
commission ("commission') considered to be "planning commissioners,,
under the Act and, therefore, required to have their statements of economic
interests forwarded to the Fair Political Practices commission ("Fppc,)?
2. Must members of the newly formed commission file annual statements of
economic interests on April 7,2003?
CONCLUSION
l. The members of the commission are considered to be planning commissioners
and their statements of economic interests must be forwarded to the FPPC.
2. Planning commissioners are required to file annual statements of economic
interests due April 1 of each year. However, an exception to this rule allows
those officials who assume office between October 1 and December 31 and
file assuming office statements of economic interests to wait to file an annual
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
@E


File No. A-02-332
Page No. 2
statement until the following year. If members of the commission meet the
criteria discussed below, they will not file an annual statement until April l,
2004.
FACTS
The City Council of Monte Sereno has appointed four residents to serve on a
newly reorgarized commission, the Site and Architectural Commission. Previously, this
commission was comprised of two council members. The new commission will now
meet on a monthly basis to review plans of proposed new residential conskuction or
residential additions/remodels. It is the charge of this commission to make the required
findings whether to approve, conditionally approve or deny the proposal. The
commission's decision, unless appealed to the city council, is final.
Monte Sereno is an all-residential community and has no other commissions. The
Monte Sereno Site and Architecture Commission has no authority to adopt, delete or
otherwise modifr the city's zoningregulations. This commission merely reviews
residential construction proposals for design and neighborhood compatibility.
AI\ALYSIS
While many types ofpublic officials file statements of economic interests in
accordance with conflict of interest codes promulgated by their respective agencies
(sections 87300 et seq.), sections 87200 - 87210 set out the financial interest reporting
obligations under which certain specified public officials, including members of planning
commissions, must report all of their economic interests. Although the definition of
"planning commission" is not found in the Act, we have previously advised that the
members of all planning commissions organized under Government Code section 65100
are "planning commissioners" within the meaning of sections 87200 - 87210. (West
Advice Lette4 No. 4-82-162.) You confirmed in a telephone conversation that the
commission is organized under section 65100.'z Accordingly, it seems appropriate that
the members of the City of Monte Sereno Site and Architecfure Commission are de facto
planning commissioners under the Act. Section 87500(9) requires planning
commissioners to file original statements of economic interests with their agencies
"which shall make and retain a copy and forward the original to the [FPPC] which shall
be the filing officer."
In answer to your question concerning whether or not the commission members
must file annual statements due April 1,2003, regulation 18723(b) requires that planning
commissioners file a statement of economic interests on or by April 1 of each year.
However, subsection (c) of regulation 18723 establishes thatif aperson assumes an
office specified in section 87200 between October 1 and December 31 and files an
assuming office statement of economic interests pursuant to section 87202,that person
need not file an annual statement of economic interests pursuant to section 87203 tntil
' Please note that the FPPC does not act as a finder offact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby
(1975) 1 FPPC Ops.71,77.)


File No. A-02-332
Page No. 3
one year later. (Regulation 18723(c).) Anticipating that the commission members would
be considered planning commissioners and, therefore, filers under section B7zOO,you had
requested that each member file an assuming office statement of economic interests. If
the commissioners disclosed under the parameters of secti on 87202 (copy enclosed) and
assumed office between October I and December 31, their first annual statements of
economic interests will be due on April 1,2004, and will cover the period beginning the
day after assuming ofEce through December 3I,2003.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
--)
--=:'VcC ca.a4 2
By: Adrianne Korchmaros
N e[< uuaA N
Political Reform
AKje
Enclosure

statement until the following year. If members of the commission meet the

2004.
